'''六地蔵'''（ろくじぞう）とは、
* 地蔵菩薩の像を6体並べて祀った地蔵、[[地蔵菩薩#六地蔵]]を参照。 * 日本の地名 ** [[千葉県]][[長生郡]][[長柄町]]六地蔵 ** [[愛知県]][[一宮市]]今伊勢町馬寄字六地蔵 ** 愛知県[[岡崎市]]六地蔵町 ** 愛知県[[海部郡]][[七宝町]]桂字六地蔵 ** [[滋賀県]][[栗東市]]六地蔵 ** [[京都府]][[宇治市]]六地蔵 ** [[徳島県]][[那賀郡]][[那賀町]]木頭折宇字六地蔵
{{aimai}} [[Category:民間信仰|ろくしそう]] [[Category:地名|ろくしそう]]

__NOTOC__ '''六地蔵'''（ろくじぞう）、非略体: '''六地藏'''（ろくぢざう）[http://www.weblio.jp/content/%E5%85%AD%E5%9C%B0%E8%94%B5]、[http://www.weblio.jp/content/%E3%82%8D%E3%81%8F%E3%81%98%E3%81%9E%E3%81%86]とは、 * 地蔵菩薩の像を6体並べて祀った地蔵、[[地蔵菩薩#六地蔵]]を参照。 * 鉄道駅のひとつ。[[六地蔵駅]]（[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]]・[[京阪電気鉄道|京阪電鉄]]・[[京都市営地下鉄]]の駅）を参照。 * 狂言の演目、[[六地蔵 (狂言)|六地蔵]]を参照。 * 倶胝密山[[聖寶院]][[六地蔵寺]]（ろくじぞう-じ）、[[茨城県]][[水戸市]]にある[[真言宗]]の寺院 * [[江戸六地蔵]]、[[宝永]]から[[享保]]年間にかけて江戸市中の6箇所に造立された銅造地蔵菩薩坐像 * [[木津の六地蔵磨崖仏]] * [[阿波六地蔵霊場]]、[[阿波国]]（徳島県）一帯に広がる地蔵菩薩巡礼地 * [[伊予六地蔵霊場]]、[[愛媛県]]にある地蔵菩薩霊場 * [[六地蔵塔]]、[[鹿児島県]][[南さつま市]]にある石造三重塔の仏教遺跡 == 地名 == 日本の地名。 * [[千葉県]][[長生郡]][[長柄町]][[六地蔵 (長柄町)]]。もとの[[長柄郡]][[六地蔵村]]、[[長柄村 (千葉県)|長柄村]]六地蔵 * [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]][[六地蔵町 (岡崎市)|六地蔵町]]。もとの[[三河国]][[額田郡]][[岡崎六地蔵町]] * [[滋賀県]][[栗東市]][[六地蔵 (栗東市)]]。もとの[[栗太郡]][[六地蔵村]]、[[葉山村 (滋賀県)|葉山村]]六地蔵 ** [[滋賀県道116号六地蔵草津線]]（ろくじぞう くさつ-せん）、六地蔵を起点に[[草津市]][[大路三交点]]に至る一般県道 * [[京都府]][[宇治市]][[六地蔵 (宇治市)]]。京都市[[伏見区]][[桃山町 (京都市)|桃山町]][[京都市伏見区の町名|西町]]の[[浄土宗]][[法雲山]][[浄妙院]][[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]（六地蔵さん、伏見六地蔵）[http://www.kyoto-meguri.com/rokujizoumeguri.html]、[http://shigeru.kommy.com/sikioriorirokujizoumeguri.htm]に因む。もと[[紀伊郡#町村制以降の沿革|紀伊郡]][[六地蔵村]]、[[堀内村 (京都府)|堀内村]]六地蔵、のち[[宇治郡#町村制以降の沿革|宇治郡]][[宇治村 (京都府)|宇治村]]六地蔵。[[伏見区]][[桃山町 (京都市)|桃山町]]周辺の通称でもある。 ** 鉄道駅のひとつ。複数の[[六地蔵駅]]（宇治市六地蔵[[六地蔵奈良町|奈良町]]の[[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]]、伏見区桃山町[[桃山町因幡|因幡]]の[[京阪電気鉄道#未成区間|京阪電鉄]]（[[京阪電鉄六地蔵線|六地蔵線]]）、[[六地蔵奈良町|奈良町]]の[[京都市営地下鉄#営業路線の概要|京都市営地下鉄]]（[[京都市営地下鉄東西線#駅一覧|東西線]]）の駅）。 ** [[京都府道128号六地蔵停車場線]]（ろくじぞう ていしゃじょう-せん）、[[京都市]]伏見区[[京阪六地蔵停車場]]を起点に伏見区六地蔵[[交点]]に至る一般府道 * [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]][[六地蔵 (豊岡市)|六地蔵]]。もとの[[但馬国]][[城崎郡]][[六地蔵村]]、[[三江村]]六地蔵、[[豊岡町 (兵庫県)|豊岡町]]六地蔵 ** [[兵庫県道239号下宮六地蔵線]]（しものみや ろくじぞう-せん）、豊岡市[[下宮 (豊岡市)|下宮]]と六地蔵を結ぶ一般県道 === 鉄道駅 === * [[一ノ堰六地蔵尊駅]]（いちのせき ろくじぞうそん-えき）、福島県[[会津若松市]][[門田町]]大字[[一ノ堰]]字[[一ノ堰畑中|畑中]]にある[[会津鉄道会津線]]の駅 == 関連項目 == * [[六地蔵塔]] * [[六地蔵町]] * [[笠地蔵]] * [[石仏]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{aimai}} {{DEFAULTSORT:ろくしそう}} [[Category:地蔵像]] [[Category:日本の民間信仰]] [[Category:日本の地名]] [[Category:名数6|しそう]]

__NOTOC__ '''六地蔵'''（ろくじぞう）、非略体: '''六地藏'''（ろくぢざう）[http://www.weblio.jp/content/%E5%85%AD%E5%9C%B0%E8%94%B5]、[http://www.weblio.jp/content/%E3%82%8D%E3%81%8F%E3%81%98%E3%81%9E%E3%81%86]とは、 * [[地蔵菩薩#六地蔵|六地蔵]] - 地蔵菩薩の像を6体並べて祀った地蔵。 * [[倶胝密山|俱胝密山]][[聖寶院|聖寶院]] [[水戸大師]] [[六地蔵寺|六地藏寺]]（ろくじぞう-じ） - [[茨城県]][[水戸市]][[六反田]]にある[[真言宗]]の大同2年（807年）開山の寺院、本山（末寺25寺）。[http://www.rokujizoji.or.jp/rokujizouji/ 六地蔵寺（公式）]、[http://ameblo.jp/rokujizoji/ 六地蔵寺（ブログ）]、[http://www.city.mito.lg.jp/001373/001374/0/shiteibunkazai/siteibunkazai/rokujizoujinoshidarezakura.html] * [[江戸六地蔵]] - [[宝永]]から[[享保]]年間にかけて江戸市中の6箇所に造立された銅造地蔵菩薩坐像 * [[木津の六地蔵磨崖仏]] - [[神戸市]][[西区 (神戸市)|西区]][[押部谷町]][[押部谷町木津|木津]]の六体地蔵の[[磨崖仏]] * [[阿波六地蔵霊場]] - [[阿波国]]（[[徳島県]]）一帯に広がる地蔵菩薩巡礼地 * [[伊予六地蔵霊場]] - [[伊予国]]（[[愛媛県]]）にある地蔵菩薩霊場 * [[六地蔵塔 (南さつま市)|六地蔵塔]] - [[鹿児島県]][[南さつま市]]にある石造三重塔の仏教遺跡 * [[六地蔵 (狂言)|六地蔵]] - [[狂言]]の演目[http://hibikiku.mods.jp/kyougenwiki/archives/178]、[http://www.kyogen.co.jp/outline/cat47/000425.html] == 地名 == 日本の地名。 * [[千葉県]][[長生郡]][[長柄町]] [[六地蔵 (長柄町)]] - もとの[[長柄郡]][[六地蔵村]]、[[長柄村 (千葉県)|長柄村]]六地蔵 * [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]] [[六地蔵町 (岡崎市)|六地蔵町]] - もとの[[三河国]][[額田郡]][[岡崎六地蔵町]] * [[滋賀県]][[栗東市]] [[六地蔵 (栗東市)]] - もとの[[栗太郡]]六地蔵村、[[葉山村 (滋賀県)|葉山村]]六地蔵 * [[京都府]][[宇治市]] [[六地蔵 (宇治市)]] - 京都市[[伏見区]][[桃山町 (京都市)|桃山町]]西町の[[浄土宗]][[法雲山]][[浄妙院]][[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]（六地蔵さん、伏見六地蔵）[http://www.kyoto-meguri.com/rokujizoumeguri.html 京の六地蔵巡り]、[http://shigeru.kommy.com/sikioriorirokujizoumeguri.htm 京の六地蔵巡り]に因む。もと[[紀伊郡]]六地蔵村、[[堀内村 (京都府)|堀内村]]六地蔵、のち[[宇治郡]][[宇治村 (京都府)|宇治村]]六地蔵。伏見区桃山町周辺の通称でもある。 * [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]] [[六地蔵 (豊岡市)|六地蔵]] - もとの[[但馬国]][[城崎郡]]六地蔵村、[[三江村]]六地蔵、[[豊岡町 (兵庫県)|豊岡町]]六地蔵 == 鉄道駅 == * [[六地蔵駅]] - [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]]・[[京阪電気鉄道|京阪電鉄]]・[[京都市営地下鉄]]の駅。 * [[一ノ堰六地蔵尊駅]]（いちのせき ろくじぞうそん-えき） - 福島県[[会津若松市]]にある[[会津鉄道会津線]]の駅。 == 関連項目 == * [[六地蔵塔]] * [[六地蔵町]] * [[笠地蔵]] * [[六道]] * [[石仏]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{aimai}} {{デフォルトソート:ろくしそう}} [[Category:地蔵像]] [[Category:日本の民間信仰]] [[Category:日本の地名]] [[Category:名数6|しそう]]

'''六地蔵'''（ろくじぞう）
* [[六道]]の思想に基づいて[[地蔵菩薩]]の像を6体並べて祀ったもの。[[六体地蔵]] ** [[六地蔵塔]] - 側面に6体の地蔵像が彫られた石塔 ** [[六地蔵寺]] - 茨城県水戸市にある真言宗の寺院。6体の地蔵菩薩を本尊とする ** [[江戸六地蔵]] - 宝永から享保年間にかけて江戸の6か所に造立された地蔵菩薩 ** [[木津の磨崖仏|木津の六地蔵磨崖仏]] - 兵庫県神戸市西区にある6体の地蔵の磨崖仏 ** [[阿波六地蔵霊場]] - 徳島県（阿波国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場 ** [[伊予六地蔵霊場]] - 愛媛県（伊予国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場 * [[六地蔵 (狂言)]] - [[狂言]]の演目 * 日本の地名 ** [[六地蔵町 (岡崎市)]] - 愛知県岡崎市の地名 ** 千葉県長生郡[[長柄町]]六地蔵 ** 滋賀県[[栗東市]]六地蔵 ** 京都府[[宇治市]]六地蔵。京都市伏見区にある[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]（伏見六地蔵）に由来する *** [[六地蔵駅]] - 京都府にある西日本旅客鉄道（JR西日本）・京阪電気鉄道・京都市営地下鉄の駅 ** 兵庫県[[豊岡市]]六地蔵
== 関連項目 == * {{Prefix}}
{{aimai}} {{デフォルトソート:ろくしそう}} [[Category:地蔵像]] [[Category:日本の民間信仰]] [[Category:日本の地名]] [[Category:名数6|しそう]]

もとの[[長柄郡]][[六地蔵村]]、[[長柄村 (千葉県)|長柄村]]六地蔵 * [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]] [[六地蔵町 (岡崎市)|六地蔵町]] - もとの[[三河国]][[額田郡]][[岡崎六地蔵町]] * [[滋賀県]][[栗東市]] [[六地蔵 (栗東市)]] - もとの[[栗太郡]]六地蔵村、[[葉山村 (滋賀県)|葉山村]]六地蔵 * [[京都府]][[宇治市]] [[六地蔵 (宇治市)]] - もと[[紀伊郡]]六地蔵村の一部（[[山科川]]以東）、のち[[宇治郡]][[宇治村 (京都府)|宇治村]]六地蔵。もと紀伊郡[[堀内村 (京都府)|堀内村]]六地蔵（紀伊郡六地蔵村の山科川以西）であった伏見区[[桃山]]地区の一部（[[桃山丘陵]]以外）の通称でもあり、京都市[[伏見区]][[桃山町 (京都市)|桃山町]]西町の[[浄土宗]][[法雲山]][[浄妙院]][[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]（六地蔵さん、伏見六地蔵）[http://www.kyoto-meguri.com/rokujizoumeguri.html 京の六地蔵巡り]、[http://shigeru.kommy.com/sikioriorirokujizoumeguri.htm 京の六地蔵巡り]に因む。 * [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]] [[六地蔵 (豊岡市)|六地蔵]] - もとの[[但馬国]][[城崎郡]]六地蔵村、[[三江村]]六地蔵、[[豊岡町 (兵庫県)|豊岡町]]六地蔵 == 日本の主な鉄道駅名 == * [[六地蔵駅]] - [[西日本旅客鉄道|JR西日本]]・[[京阪電気鉄道|京阪電鉄]]・[[京都市営地下鉄]]の駅。 * [[一ノ堰六地蔵尊駅]]（いちのせき ろくじぞうそん-えき） - 福島県[[会津若松市]]にある[[会津鉄道会津線]]の駅。 == 関連項目 == * [[六地蔵塔]] * [[六地蔵町]] * [[笠地蔵]] * [[六道]] * [[石仏]] == 脚注 == {{Reflist}} {{aimai}} {{デフォルトソート:ろくしそう}} [[Category:地蔵像]] [[Category:日本の民間信仰]] [[Category:日本の地名]] [[Category:名数6|しそう]] [[Category:通称]]

'''六地蔵'''（ろくじぞう）
* [[六道]]の思想に基づいて[[地蔵菩薩]]の像を6体並べて祀ったもの。[[六体地蔵]]。 ** [[六地蔵塔]] - 側面に6体の地蔵像が彫られた石塔 ** [[六地蔵寺]] - 茨城県水戸市にある真言宗の寺院。6体の地蔵菩薩を本尊とする ** [[江戸六地蔵]] - [[宝永]]から[[享保]]年間にかけて江戸の6か所に造立された銅像地蔵菩薩像の総称 ** [[京都六地蔵]] - 京都市内6か所に祀られている木像地蔵菩薩像の総称 ** [[木津の六地蔵磨崖仏]] - 兵庫県神戸市西区にある6体の地蔵の[[磨崖仏]] ** [[阿波六地蔵霊場]] - 徳島県（阿波国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場 ** [[伊予六地蔵霊場]] - 愛媛県（伊予国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場 * [[六地蔵 (狂言)]] - [[狂言]]の演目 * 日本の地名 ** [[六地蔵 (長柄町)]] - [[千葉県]][[長生郡]][[長柄町]]の地名 ** [[六地蔵 (栗東市)]] - [[滋賀県]][[栗東市]]の地名 ** [[六地蔵 (宇治市)]] - [[京都府]][[宇治市]]の地名。京都市伏見区にある[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]（伏見六地蔵）に由来する *** [[六地蔵駅]] - 京都府にある西日本旅客鉄道（JR西日本）・京阪電気鉄道・京都市営地下鉄の駅 ** [[六地蔵 (豊岡市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名 ** [[六地蔵町 (岡崎市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]]の地名

* [[六道]]の思想に基づいて[[地蔵菩薩]]の像を6体並べて祀ったもの。[[六体地蔵]]。 ** [[六地蔵塔]] - 側面に6体の地蔵像が彫られた石塔 ** [[六地蔵寺]] - 茨城県水戸市にある真言宗の寺院。6体の地蔵菩薩を本尊とする **[[京都六地蔵]] - [[六地蔵村]]の[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]に祀られていたものを[[保元]]年間の[[平清盛]]の命で京の街道口6ヶ所に分置した6体の木像地蔵菩薩像の総称 ** [[江戸六地蔵]] - 京の六地蔵に倣って[[宝永]]から[[享保]]年間にかけて江戸の6か所に造立された銅像地蔵菩薩像の総称 ** [[木津の六地蔵磨崖仏]] - 兵庫県神戸市西区にある6体の地蔵の[[磨崖仏]] ** [[京大坂道|高野街道]]六地蔵 - 和歌山県の高野街道沿いに江戸時代に設置された *** 第1の地蔵：橋本市清水地区 *** 第2の地蔵：橋本市南馬場地区 *** 第3の地蔵：九度山町繁野地区 *** 第4の地蔵：九度山町河根地区 *** 第5の地蔵：高野町作水地区 *** 第6の地蔵：高野町桜茶屋地区 ** [[阿波六地蔵霊場]] - 徳島県（阿波国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場 ** [[伊予六地蔵霊場]] - 愛媛県（伊予国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場 * [[六地蔵 (狂言)]] - [[狂言]]の演目 * 日本の地名 **[[六地蔵 (長柄町)]] - [[千葉県]][[長生郡]][[長柄町]]の地名 ** [[六地蔵町 (岡崎市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]]の地名 ** [[六地蔵 (栗東市)]] - [[滋賀県]][[栗東市]]の地名 ** [[六地蔵村]] - [[山城国]][[紀伊郡]]（[[京都府]]）にかつて存在した村。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]（伏見六地蔵・[[京都市]][[伏見区]][[京都市伏見区の町名#旧堀内村|桃山町]]）に祀られていた6体の木像地蔵菩薩像に由来する。 ** [[六地蔵 (宇治市)]] - 京都府[[宇治市]]の地名。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]周辺に存在した[[六地蔵村]]の[[山科川]]以東の地区。 *** [[六地蔵駅]] - 京都府にある西日本旅客鉄道（JR西日本）・京阪電気鉄道・京都市営地下鉄の駅 ** [[六地蔵 (豊岡市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名

[[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[ja:%E5%85%AD%E5%9C%B0%E8%94%B5]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[en:Six Jizō]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[en:Six Jizō]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[en:Six Jizō]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[en:Six Jizō]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[en:Six Jizō]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:100 shortest pages as of February 11 2025]] [[Category:redirect]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] '''六地蔵'''（ろくじぞう）
* [[六道]]の思想に基づいて[[地蔵菩薩]]の像を6体並べて祀ったもの。[[六体地蔵]]。 ** [[六地蔵塔]] - 側面に6体の地蔵像が彫られた石塔。 ** [[六地蔵寺]] - 茨城県水戸市にある真言宗の寺院。6体の地蔵菩薩を本尊とする。 **[[京都六地蔵]] - [[852年]]（[[仁寿]]2年）、[[小野篁]]（おの の たかむら）が桜の大木を用いて彫ったとされ、[[六地蔵村]]の[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]に祀られていたものを、[[保元]]年間の[[平清盛]]の命で京の街道口6ヶ所に分置した、6体の木像地蔵菩薩像の総称。 ** [[江戸六地蔵]] - 京の六地蔵に倣って[[宝永]]から[[享保]]年間にかけて江戸の6か所に造立された銅像地蔵菩薩像の総称。 ** [[木津の六地蔵磨崖仏]] - 兵庫県神戸市西区にある6体の地蔵の[[磨崖仏]]。 ** [[京大坂道|高野街道]]六地蔵 - 和歌山県の高野街道沿いに江戸時代に設置された。 *** 第1の地蔵：橋本市清水地区 *** 第2の地蔵：橋本市南馬場地区 *** 第3の地蔵：九度山町繁野地区 *** 第4の地蔵：九度山町河根地区 *** 第5の地蔵：高野町作水地区 *** 第6の地蔵：高野町桜茶屋地区 ** [[阿波六地蔵霊場]] - 徳島県（阿波国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場。 ** [[伊予六地蔵霊場]] - 愛媛県（伊予国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場。 * [[六地蔵 (狂言)]] - [[狂言]]の演目
; 日本の地名 * [[六地蔵 (長柄町)]] - [[千葉県]][[長生郡]][[長柄町]]の地名 * [[六地蔵町 (岡崎市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]]の地名 * [[六地蔵 (栗東市)]] - [[滋賀県]][[栗東市]]の地名 * [[六地蔵村]] - [[山城国]][[紀伊郡]]（[[京都府]]）にかつて存在した村。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]（伏見六地蔵・[[京都市]][[伏見区]][[京都市伏見区の町名#旧堀内村|桃山町]]）に祀られていた6体の木像地蔵菩薩像に由来する。 * [[六地蔵 (宇治市)]] - 京都府[[宇治市]]の地名。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]周辺に存在した[[六地蔵村]]の[[山科川]]以東の地区。 * [[六地蔵 (豊岡市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名
; 駅名 * [[六地蔵駅]] - 京都府京都市伏見区と宇治市にまたがる地域にある西日本旅客鉄道（JR西日本）・京阪電気鉄道・京都市営地下鉄の駅
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Six Jizo}} * {{Prefix}} * {{Intitle}}
{{aimai}}
{{デフォルトソート:ろくしそう}} [[Category:仏教の名数6|しそう]] [[Category:日本の地名]] [[Category:同名の地名]]

; 日本の地名 * [[六地蔵 (長柄町)]] - [[千葉県]][[長生郡]][[長柄町]]の地名 * [[六地蔵町 (岡崎市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]]の地名 * [[六地蔵 (栗東市)]] - [[滋賀県]][[栗東市]]の地名 * [[六地蔵村]] - [[山城国]][[紀伊郡]]（[[京都府]]）にかつて存在した村。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]（伏見六地蔵・[[京都市]][[伏見区]][[京都市伏見区の町名#旧堀内村|桃山町]]）に祀られていた6体の木像地蔵菩薩像に由来する。 * [[六地蔵 (宇治市)]] - 京都府[[宇治市]]の地名。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]周辺に存在した[[六地蔵村]]の[[山科川]]以東の地区。 * [[六地蔵 (豊岡市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名
; 駅名 * [[六地蔵駅]] - 京都府京都市伏見区と宇治市にまたがる地域にある西日本旅客鉄道（JR西日本）・京阪電気鉄道・京都市営地下鉄の駅

* [[六道]]の思想に基づいて[[地蔵菩薩]]の像を6体並べて祀ったもの。[[六体地蔵]]。 ** [[六地蔵塔]] - 側面に6体の地蔵像が彫られた石塔。 ** [[六地蔵寺]] - 茨城県水戸市にある真言宗の寺院。6体の地蔵菩薩を本尊とする。 **[[京都六地蔵]] - [[852]]（[[仁寿]]2年）、[[小野篁]]（おの の たかむら）が桜の大木を用いて彫ったとされ、[[六地蔵村]]の[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]に祀られていたものを、[[保元]]年間の[[平清盛]]の命で京の街道口6ヶ所に分置した、6体の木像地蔵菩薩像の総称。 ** [[江戸六地蔵]] - 京の六地蔵に倣って[[宝永]]から[[享保]]年間にかけて江戸の6か所に造立された銅像地蔵菩薩像の総称。 ** [[木津の六地蔵磨崖仏]] - 兵庫県神戸市西区にある6体の地蔵の[[磨崖仏]]。 ** [[京大坂道|高野街道]]六地蔵 - 和歌山県の高野街道沿いに江戸時代に設置された。 *** 第1の地蔵：橋本市清水地区 *** 第2の地蔵：橋本市南馬場地区 *** 第3の地蔵：九度山町繁野地区 *** 第4の地蔵：九度山町河根地区 *** 第5の地蔵：高野町作水地区 *** 第6の地蔵：高野町桜茶屋地区 ** [[阿波六地蔵霊場]] - 徳島県（阿波国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場。 ** [[伊予六地蔵霊場]] - 愛媛県（伊予国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場。 * [[六地蔵 (狂言)]] - [[狂言]]の演目
; 日本の地名 * [[六地蔵 (長柄町)]] - [[千葉県]][[長生郡]][[長柄町]]の地名 * [[六地蔵町 (岡崎市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]]の地名 * [[六地蔵 (栗東市)]] - [[滋賀県]][[栗東市]]の地名 * [[六地蔵村]] - [[山城国]][[紀伊郡]]（[[京都府]]）にかつて存在した村。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]（伏見六地蔵・[[京都市]][[伏見区]][[京都市伏見区の町名#旧堀内村|桃山町]]）に祀られていた6体の木像地蔵菩薩像に由来する。 * [[六地蔵 (宇治市)]] - 京都府[[宇治市]]の地名。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]周辺に存在した[[六地蔵村]]の[[山科川]]以東の地区。 * [[六地蔵 (豊岡市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名
; 駅名 * [[六地蔵駅]] - 京都府京都市伏見区と宇治市にまたがる地域にある西日本旅客鉄道（JR西日本）・京阪電気鉄道・京都市営地下鉄の駅

{{aimai}}

; 日本の地名 * [[六地蔵 (長柄町)]] - [[千葉県]][[長生郡]][[長柄町]]の地名 * [[六地蔵町 (岡崎市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]]の地名 * [[六地蔵 (栗東市)]] - [[滋賀県]][[栗東市]]の地名 * [[六地蔵村]] - [[山城国]][[紀伊郡]]（[[京都府]]）にかつて存在した村。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]（伏見六地蔵・[[京都市]][[伏見区]][[京都市伏見区の町名#旧堀内村|桃山町]]）に祀られていた6体の木像地蔵菩薩像に由来する。 * [[六地蔵 (宇治市)]] - 京都府[[宇治市]]の地名。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]周辺に存在した[[六地蔵村]]の[[山科川]]以東の地区。 * [[六地蔵 (豊岡市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名
; 駅名 * [[六地蔵駅]] - 京都府京都市伏見区と宇治市にまたがる地域にある西日本旅客鉄道（JR西日本）・京阪電気鉄道・京都市営地下鉄の駅

[[ja:%E5%85%AD%E5%9C%B0%E8%94%B5]] [[en:Six Jizō]] [[en:Six Jizō]] [[en:Six Jizō]] [[en:Six Jizō]] [[en:Six Jizō]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Pages of uncertain originality]] '''六地蔵'''（ろくじぞう）
* [[六道]]の思想に基づいて[[地蔵菩薩]]の像を6体並べて祀ったもの。[[六体地蔵]]。 ** [[六地蔵塔]] - 側面に6体の地蔵像が彫られた石塔。 ** [[六地蔵寺]] - 茨城県水戸市にある真言宗の寺院。6体の地蔵菩薩を本尊とする。 **[[京都六地蔵]] - [[852]]（[[仁寿]]2年）、[[小野篁]]（おの の たかむら）が桜の大木を用いて彫ったとされ、[[六地蔵村]]の[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]に祀られていたものを、[[保元]]年間の[[平清盛]]の命で京の街道口6ヶ所に分置した、6体の木像地蔵菩薩像の総称。 ** [[江戸六地蔵]] - 京の六地蔵に倣って[[宝永]]から[[享保]]年間にかけて江戸の6か所に造立された銅像地蔵菩薩像の総称。 ** [[木津の六地蔵磨崖仏]] - 兵庫県神戸市西区にある6体の地蔵の[[磨崖仏]]。 ** [[京大坂道|高野街道]]六地蔵 - 和歌山県の高野街道沿いに江戸時代に設置された。 *** 第1の地蔵：橋本市清水地区 *** 第2の地蔵：橋本市南馬場地区 *** 第3の地蔵：九度山町繁野地区 *** 第4の地蔵：九度山町河根地区 *** 第5の地蔵：高野町作水地区 *** 第6の地蔵：高野町桜茶屋地区 ** [[阿波六地蔵霊場]] - 徳島県（阿波国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場。 ** [[伊予六地蔵霊場]] - 愛媛県（伊予国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場。 * [[六地蔵 (狂言)]] - [[狂言]]の演目
; 日本の地名 * [[六地蔵 (長柄町)]] - [[千葉県]][[長生郡]][[長柄町]]の地名 * [[六地蔵町 (岡崎市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]]の地名 * [[六地蔵 (栗東市)]] - [[滋賀県]][[栗東市]]の地名 * [[六地蔵村]] - [[Yamashiro Province ]][[紀伊郡]]（[[京都府]]）にかつて存在した村。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]（伏見六地蔵・[[京都市]][[伏見区]][[京都市伏見区の町名#旧堀内村|桃山町]]）に祀られていた6体の木像地蔵菩薩像に由来する。 * [[六地蔵 (宇治市)]] - 京都府[[宇治市]]の地名。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]周辺に存在した[[六地蔵村]]の[[山科川]]以東の地区。 * [[六地蔵 (豊岡市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名
; 駅名 * [[六地蔵駅]] - 京都府京都市伏見区と宇治市にまたがる地域にある西日本旅客鉄道（JR西日本）・京阪電気鉄道・京都市営地下鉄の駅
== See Also == {{Commonscat|Six Jizo}} * {{Prefix}} * {{Intitle}}
{{aimai}}
{{citation needed|ろくしそう}} [[Category:仏教の名数6|しそう]] [[Category:日本の地名]] [[Category:同名の地名]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Six Jizō]][[Category:11]]

revision.text:
'''Six Jizō''' (六地蔵, Rokujizō)
* Refers to six statues of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩}} enshrined together, based on the Buddhist concept of the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道}}. Also called {{ill|Rokutai Jizō|ja|六体地蔵}}. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-tō|ja|六地蔵塔}} – Stone towers carved with six Jizō images on their sides. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-ji|ja|六地蔵寺}} – A Shingon sect temple in Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture, enshrining six Jizō Bosatsu as its principal images. ** {{ill|Kyōto Rokujizō|ja|京都六地蔵}} – A set of six wooden Jizō Bosatsu statues said to have been carved from a large cherry tree by {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁}} in 852 (Ninju 2). Originally enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)}} in {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村}}, they were redistributed during the Hōgen era to six entrance points to the capital by order of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛}}. ** {{ill|Edo Rokujizō|ja|江戸六地蔵}} – Modeled after the Kyoto set, these are six copper Jizō statues constructed at various places in Edo between the Hōei and Kyōhō eras. ** {{ill|Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja|木津の六地蔵磨崖仏}} – Six carved Jizō figures on rock faces in Nishi-ku, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. ** {{ill|Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja|高野街道六地蔵}} – Jizō statues established along the Kōya Kaidō route in Wakayama Prefecture during the Edo period. *** First Jizō: Shimizu area, Hashimoto City *** Second Jizō: Minamibaba area, Hashimoto City *** Third Jizō: Shigeno area, Kudoyama Town *** Fourth Jizō: Kawane area, Kudoyama Town *** Fifth Jizō: Sakumizu area, Kōya Town *** Sixth Jizō: Sakurachaya area, Kōya Town ** {{ill|Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|阿波六地蔵霊場}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Tokushima Prefecture (Awa Province). ** {{ill|Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|伊予六地蔵霊場}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Ehime Prefecture (Iyo Province). * {{ill|Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja|六地蔵 (狂言)}} – A traditional Kyōgen play titled "Rokujizō."
; Place names in Japan * {{ill|Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja|六地蔵 (長柄町)}} – A locality in Nagara Town, Chōsei District, Chiba Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|ja|六地蔵町 (岡崎市)}} – A locality in Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja|六地蔵 (栗東市)}} – A locality in Rittō City, Shiga Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村}} – A former village in Kii District, Yamashiro Province (now Kyoto Prefecture). Associated with six wooden Jizō statues enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)}}, located in Momoyama-chō, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Uji)|ja|六地蔵 (宇治市)}} – A locality in Uji City, Kyoto Prefecture. Lies east of Yamashina River in the area historically part of Rokujizō Village. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja|六地蔵 (豊岡市)}} – A locality in Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture
; Stations * {{ill|Rokujizō Station|ja|六地蔵駅}} – A station located on the border of Fushimi Ward (Kyoto City) and Uji City, operated by JR West, Keihan Electric Railway, and Kyoto Municipal Subway.
==See Also== {{Commonscat|Six Jizo}} * {{Prefix|Rokujizō}} * {{Intitle|Rokujizō}}
{{Disambiguation}} {{citation needed|ろくしそう}}

; Place names in Japan * {{ill|Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja|六地蔵 (長柄町)}} – A locality in Nagara Town, Chōsei District, Chiba Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|ja|六地蔵町 (岡崎市)}} – A locality in Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja|六地蔵 (栗東市)}} – A locality in Rittō City, Shiga Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村}} – A former village in Kii District, Yamashiro Province (now Kyoto Prefecture). Associated with six wooden Jizō statues enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)}}, located in Momoyama-chō, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Uji)|ja|六地蔵 (宇治市)}} – A locality in Uji City, Kyoto Prefecture. Lies east of Yamashina River in the area historically part of Rokujizō Village. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja|六地蔵 (豊岡市)}} – A locality in Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture
; Stations * {{ill|Rokujizō Station|ja|六地蔵駅}} – A station located on the border of Fushimi Ward (Kyoto City) and Uji City, operated by JR West, Keihan Electric Railway, and Kyoto Municipal Subway.

Associated with six wooden Jizō statues enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=draft}}, located in Momoyama-chō, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Uji)|ja|六地蔵 (宇治市)|12=draft}} – A locality in Uji City, Kyoto Prefecture. Lies east of Yamashina River in the area historically part of Rokujizō Village. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja|六地蔵 (豊岡市)|12=draft}} – A locality in Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture
; Stations * {{ill|Rokujizō Station|ja|六地蔵駅|12=draft}} – A station located on the border of Fushimi Ward (Kyoto City) and Uji City, operated by JR West, Keihan Electric Railway, and Kyoto Municipal Subway.

'''Six Jizō''' (六地蔵, Rokujizō)
* Refers to six statues of {{ill|Jizō Bosatsu|ja|地蔵菩薩|12=draft}} enshrined together, based on the Buddhist concept of the {{ill|Six Realms of Existence|ja|六道|12=draft}}. Also called {{ill|Rokutai Jizō|ja|六体地蔵|12=draft}}. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-tō|ja|六地蔵塔|12=draft}} – Stone towers carved with six Jizō images on their sides. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-ji|ja|六地蔵寺|12=draft}} – A Shingon sect temple in Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture, enshrining six Jizō Bosatsu as its principal images. ** {{ill|Kyōto Rokujizō|ja|京都六地蔵|12=draft}} – A set of six wooden Jizō Bosatsu statues said to have been carved from a large cherry tree by {{ill|Ono no Takamura|ja|小野篁|12=draft}} in 852 (Ninju 2). Originally enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=draft}} in {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=draft}}, they were redistributed during the Hōgen era to six entrance points to the capital by order of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|ja|平清盛|12=draft}}. ** {{ill|Edo Rokujizō|ja|江戸六地蔵|12=draft}} – Modeled after the Kyoto set, these are six copper Jizō statues constructed at various places in Edo between the Hōei and Kyōhō eras. ** {{ill|Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja|木津の六地蔵磨崖仏|12=draft}} – Six carved Jizō figures on rock faces in Nishi-ku, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. ** {{ill|Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja|高野街道六地蔵|12=draft}} – Jizō statues established along the Kōya Kaidō route in Wakayama Prefecture during the Edo period. *** First Jizō: Shimizu area, Hashimoto City *** Second Jizō: Minamibaba area, Hashimoto City *** Third Jizō: Shigeno area, Kudoyama Town *** Fourth Jizō: Kawane area, Kudoyama Town *** Fifth Jizō: Sakumizu area, Kōya Town *** Sixth Jizō: Sakurachaya area, Kōya Town ** {{ill|Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|阿波六地蔵霊場|12=draft}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Tokushima Prefecture (Awa Province). ** {{ill|Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|伊予六地蔵霊場|12=draft}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Ehime Prefecture (Iyo Province). * {{ill|Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja|六地蔵 (狂言)|12=draft}} – A traditional Kyōgen play titled "Rokujizō."
; Place names in Japan * {{ill|Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja|六地蔵 (長柄町)|12=draft}} – A locality in Nagara Town, Chōsei District, Chiba Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|ja|六地蔵町 (岡崎市)|12=draft}} – A locality in Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja|六地蔵 (栗東市)|12=draft}} – A locality in Rittō City, Shiga Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=draft}} – A former village in Kii District, Yamashiro Province (now Kyoto Prefecture). Associated with six wooden Jizō statues enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=draft}}, located in Momoyama-chō, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Uji)|ja|六地蔵 (宇治市)|12=draft}} – A locality in Uji City, Kyoto Prefecture. Lies east of Yamashina River in the area historically part of Rokujizō Village. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja|六地蔵 (豊岡市)|12=draft}} – A locality in Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture
; Stations * {{ill|Rokujizō Station|ja|六地蔵駅|12=draft}} – A station located on the border of Fushimi Ward (Kyoto City) and Uji City, operated by JR West, Keihan Electric Railway, and Kyoto Municipal Subway.

51 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:六地蔵 up to rev 98537813
revision.text:
'''六地蔵'''（ろくじぞう）
* [[六道]]の思想に基づいて[[地蔵菩薩]]の像を6体並べて祀ったもの。[[六体地蔵]]。 ** [[六地蔵塔]] - 側面に6体の地蔵像が彫られた石塔。 ** [[六地蔵寺]] - 茨城県水戸市にある真言宗の寺院。6体の地蔵菩薩を本尊とする。 **[[京都六地蔵]] - [[852年]]（[[仁寿]]2年）、[[小野篁]]（おの の たかむら）が桜の大木を用いて彫ったとされ、[[六地蔵村]]の[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]に祀られていたものを、[[保元]]年間の[[平清盛]]の命で京の街道口6ヶ所に分置した、6体の木像地蔵菩薩像の総称。 ** [[江戸六地蔵]] - 京の六地蔵に倣って[[宝永]]から[[享保]]年間にかけて江戸の6か所に造立された銅像地蔵菩薩像の総称。 ** [[木津の六地蔵磨崖仏]] - 兵庫県神戸市西区にある6体の地蔵の[[磨崖仏]]。 ** [[京大坂道|高野街道]]六地蔵 - 和歌山県の高野街道沿いに江戸時代に設置された。 *** 第1の地蔵：橋本市清水地区 *** 第2の地蔵：橋本市南馬場地区 *** 第3の地蔵：九度山町繁野地区 *** 第4の地蔵：九度山町河根地区 *** 第5の地蔵：高野町作水地区 *** 第6の地蔵：高野町桜茶屋地区 ** [[阿波六地蔵霊場]] - 徳島県（阿波国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場。 ** [[伊予六地蔵霊場]] - 愛媛県（伊予国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場。 * [[六地蔵 (狂言)]] - [[狂言]]の演目
; 日本の地名 * [[六地蔵 (長柄町)]] - [[千葉県]][[長生郡]][[長柄町]]の地名 * [[六地蔵町 (岡崎市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]]の地名 * [[六地蔵 (栗東市)]] - [[滋賀県]][[栗東市]]の地名 * [[六地蔵村]] - [[山城国]][[紀伊郡]]（[[京都府]]）にかつて存在した村。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]（伏見六地蔵・[[京都市]][[伏見区]][[京都市伏見区の町名#旧堀内村|桃山町]]）に祀られていた6体の木像地蔵菩薩像に由来する。 * [[六地蔵 (宇治市)]] - 京都府[[宇治市]]の地名。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]周辺に存在した[[六地蔵村]]の[[山科川]]以東の地区。 * [[六地蔵 (豊岡市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名
; 駅名 * [[六地蔵駅]] - 京都府京都市伏見区と宇治市にまたがる地域にある西日本旅客鉄道（JR西日本）・京阪電気鉄道・京都市営地下鉄の駅
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Six Jizo}} * {{Prefix}} * {{Intitle}}

'''六地蔵'''（ろくじぞう）
* [[六道]]の思想に基づいて[[地蔵菩薩]]の像を6体並べて祀ったもの。[[六体地蔵]]。 ** [[六地蔵塔]] - 側面に6体の地蔵像が彫られた石塔。 ** [[六地蔵寺]] - 茨城県水戸市にある真言宗の寺院。6体の地蔵菩薩を本尊とする。 **[[京都六地蔵]] - [[852年]]（[[仁寿]]2年）、[[小野篁]]（おの の たかむら）が桜の大木を用いて彫ったとされ、[[六地蔵村]]の[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]に祀られていたものを、[[保元]]年間の[[平清盛]]の命で京の街道口6ヶ所に分置した、6体の木像地蔵菩薩像の総称。 ** [[江戸六地蔵]] - 京の六地蔵に倣って[[宝永]]から[[享保]]年間にかけて江戸の6か所に造立された銅像地蔵菩薩像の総称。 ** [[木津の六地蔵磨崖仏]] - 兵庫県神戸市西区にある6体の地蔵の[[磨崖仏]]。 ** [[京大坂道|高野街道]]六地蔵 - 和歌山県の高野街道沿いに江戸時代に設置された。 *** 第1の地蔵：橋本市清水地区 *** 第2の地蔵：橋本市南馬場地区 *** 第3の地蔵：九度山町繁野地区 *** 第4の地蔵：九度山町河根地区 *** 第5の地蔵：高野町作水地区 *** 第6の地蔵：高野町桜茶屋地区 ** [[阿波六地蔵霊場]] - 徳島県（阿波国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場。 ** [[伊予六地蔵霊場]] - 愛媛県（伊予国）にある6か所の地蔵菩薩霊場。 * [[六地蔵 (狂言)]] - [[狂言]]の演目
; 日本の地名 * [[六地蔵 (長柄町)]] - [[千葉県]][[長生郡]][[長柄町]]の地名 * [[六地蔵町 (岡崎市)]] - [[愛知県]][[岡崎市]]の地名 * [[六地蔵 (栗東市)]] - [[滋賀県]][[栗東市]]の地名 * [[六地蔵村]] - [[山城国]][[紀伊郡]]（[[京都府]]）にかつて存在した村。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]（伏見六地蔵・[[京都市]][[伏見区]][[京都市伏見区の町名#旧堀内村|桃山町]]）に祀られていた6体の木像地蔵菩薩像に由来する。 * [[六地蔵 (宇治市)]] - 京都府[[宇治市]]の地名。[[大善寺 (京都市)|大善寺]]周辺に存在した[[六地蔵村]]の[[山科川]]以東の地区。 * [[六地蔵 (豊岡市)]] - [[兵庫県]][[豊岡市]]の地名
; 駅名 * [[六地蔵駅]] - 京都府京都市伏見区と宇治市にまたがる地域にある西日本旅客鉄道（JR西日本）・京阪電気鉄道・京都市営地下鉄の駅
== 関連項目 == {{Commonscat|Six Jizo}} * {{Prefix}} * {{Intitle}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11392304]] Wikidata name: Rokujizō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Six Jizō''' (六地蔵, Rokujizō)
* Refers to six statues of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] enshrined together, based on the Buddhist concept of the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]]. Also called {{ill|Rokutai Jizō|ja|六体地蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokutai Jizō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地蔵菩薩|1=Kṣitigarbha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q604687}}. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-tō|ja|六地蔵塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-tō|qq=|1=Rokujizō-tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392305}} – Stone towers carved with six Jizō images on their sides. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-ji|ja|六地蔵寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-ji|qq=|1=Rokujizo Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120882}} – A Shingon sect temple in Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture, enshrining six Jizō Bosatsu as its principal images. ** {{ill|Kyōto Rokujizō|ja|京都六地蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōto Rokujizō|qq=|1=Kyōto Rokujizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56350316}} – A set of six wooden Jizō Bosatsu statues said to have been carved from a large cherry tree by [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] in 852 (Ninju 2). Originally enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|qq=|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}} in {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|qq=|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}}, they were redistributed during the Hōgen era to six entrance points to the capital by order of [[:en:Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori]]. ** {{ill|Edo Rokujizō|ja|江戸六地蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Rokujizō|qq=|1=Edo Roku Jizo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550993}} – Modeled after the Kyoto set, these are six copper Jizō statues constructed at various places in Edo between the Hōei and Kyōhō eras. ** {{ill|Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja|木津の六地蔵磨崖仏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 木津の磨崖仏|1=Kizu Magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519200}} – Six carved Jizō figures on rock faces in Nishi-ku, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. ** {{ill|Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja|高野街道六地蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jizō statues established along the Kōya Kaidō route in Wakayama Prefecture during the Edo period. *** First Jizō: Shimizu area, Hashimoto City *** Second Jizō: Minamibaba area, Hashimoto City *** Third Jizō: Shigeno area, Kudoyama Town *** Fourth Jizō: Kawane area, Kudoyama Town *** Fifth Jizō: Sakumizu area, Kōya Town *** Sixth Jizō: Sakurachaya area, Kōya Town ** {{ill|Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|阿波六地蔵霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Awa Six jizō pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657507}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Tokushima Prefecture (Awa Province). ** {{ill|Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|伊予六地蔵霊場|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|qq=|1=Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16482459}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Ehime Prefecture (Iyo Province). * {{ill|Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja|六地蔵 (狂言)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A traditional Kyōgen play titled "Rokujizō."
; Place names in Japan * {{ill|Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja|六地蔵 (長柄町)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Nagara)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Nagara Town, Chōsei District, Chiba Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|ja|六地蔵町 (岡崎市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|qq=|1=Rokujizō-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215339}} – A locality in Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja|六地蔵 (栗東市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Rittō)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Rittō City, Shiga Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|qq=|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}} – A former village in Kii District, Yamashiro Province (now Kyoto Prefecture). Associated with six wooden Jizō statues enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|qq=|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}}, located in Momoyama-chō, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Uji)|ja|六地蔵 (宇治市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Uji)|qq=|1=Rokujizō (Uji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56349096}} – A locality in Uji City, Kyoto Prefecture. Lies east of Yamashina River in the area historically part of Rokujizō Village. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja|六地蔵 (豊岡市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Toyooka)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture
; Stations * {{ill|Rokujizō Station|ja|六地蔵駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Station|qq=}} – A station located on the border of Fushimi Ward (Kyoto City) and Uji City, operated by JR West, Keihan Electric Railway, and Kyoto Municipal Subway.

* Refers to six statues of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] enshrined together, based on the Buddhist concept of the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]]. Also called {{ill|Rokutai Jizō|ja|六体地蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokutai Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地蔵菩薩|1=Kṣitigarbha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q604687}}. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-tō|ja|六地蔵塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-tō|1=Rokujizō-tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392305}} – Stone towers carved with six Jizō images on their sides. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-ji|ja|六地蔵寺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-ji|1=Rokujizo Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120882}} – A Shingon sect temple in Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture, enshrining six Jizō Bosatsu as its principal images. ** {{ill|Kyōto Rokujizō|ja|京都六地蔵|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōto Rokujizō|1=Kyōto Rokujizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56350316}} – A set of six wooden Jizō Bosatsu statues said to have been carved from a large cherry tree by [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] in 852 (Ninju 2). Originally enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}} in {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}}, they were redistributed revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392304]] Wikidata name: Rokujizō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Six Jizō''' (六地蔵, Rokujizō)
* Refers to six statues of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] enshrined together, based on the Buddhist concept of the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]]. Also called {{ill|Rokutai Jizō|ja|六体地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokutai Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地蔵菩薩|1=Kṣitigarbha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q604687}}. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-tō|ja|六地蔵塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-tō|1=Rokujizō-tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392305}} – Stone towers carved with six Jizō images on their sides. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-ji|ja|六地蔵寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-ji|1=Rokujizo Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120882}} – A Shingon sect temple in Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture, enshrining six Jizō Bosatsu as its principal images. ** {{ill|Kyōto Rokujizō|ja|京都六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōto Rokujizō|1=Kyōto Rokujizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56350316}} – A set of six wooden Jizō Bosatsu statues said to have been carved from a large cherry tree by [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] in 852 (Ninju 2). Originally enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}} in {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}}, they were redistributed during the Hōgen era to six entrance points to the capital by order of [[:en:Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori]]. ** {{ill|Edo Rokujizō|ja|江戸六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Rokujizō|1=Edo Roku Jizo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550993}} – Modeled after the Kyoto set, these are six copper Jizō statues constructed at various places in Edo between the Hōei and Kyōhō eras. ** {{ill|Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja|木津の六地蔵磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 木津の磨崖仏|1=Kizu Magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519200}} – Six carved Jizō figures on rock faces in Nishi-ku, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. ** {{ill|Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja|高野街道六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jizō statues established along the Kōya Kaidō route in Wakayama Prefecture during the Edo period. *** First Jizō: Shimizu area, Hashimoto City *** Second Jizō: Minamibaba area, Hashimoto City *** Third Jizō: Shigeno area, Kudoyama Town *** Fourth Jizō: Kawane area, Kudoyama Town *** Fifth Jizō: Sakumizu area, Kōya Town *** Sixth Jizō: Sakurachaya area, Kōya Town ** {{ill|Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|阿波六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Awa Six jizō pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657507}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Tokushima Prefecture (Awa Province). ** {{ill|Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|伊予六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16482459}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Ehime Prefecture (Iyo Province). * {{ill|Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja|六地蔵 (狂言)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A traditional Kyōgen play titled "Rokujizō."
; Place names in Japan * {{ill|Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja|六地蔵 (長柄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Nagara Town, Chōsei District, Chiba Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|ja|六地蔵町 (岡崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|1=Rokujizō-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215339}} – A locality in Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja|六地蔵 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Rittō City, Shiga Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}} – A former village in Kii District, Yamashiro Province (now Kyoto Prefecture). Associated with six wooden Jizō statues enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}}, located in Momoyama-chō, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Uji)|ja|六地蔵 (宇治市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Uji)|1=Rokujizō (Uji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56349096}} – A locality in Uji City, Kyoto Prefecture. Lies east of Yamashina River in the area historically part of Rokujizō Village. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja|六地蔵 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture
; Stations * {{ill|Rokujizō Station|ja|六地蔵駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Station|qq=}} – A station located on the border of Fushimi Ward (Kyoto City) and Uji City, operated by JR West, Keihan Electric Railway, and Kyoto Municipal Subway.

[[Category:Buddhist enumerations of 6]] [[Category:Place name disambiguation pages]] [[Category:Toponymy of Japan]] [[Category:Disambiguation pages]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q11392304}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{disambiguation}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392304]] Wikidata name: Rokujizō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Six Jizō''' (六地蔵, Rokujizō)
* Refers to six statues of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] enshrined together, based on the Buddhist concept of the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]]. Also called {{ill|Rokutai Jizō|ja|六体地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokutai Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地蔵菩薩|1=Kṣitigarbha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q604687}}. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-tō|ja|六地蔵塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-tō|1=Rokujizō-tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392305}} – Stone towers carved with six Jizō images on their sides. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-ji|ja|六地蔵寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-ji|1=Rokujizo Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120882}} – A Shingon sect temple in Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture, enshrining six Jizō Bosatsu as its principal images. ** {{ill|Kyōto Rokujizō|ja|京都六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōto Rokujizō|1=Kyōto Rokujizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56350316}} – A set of six wooden Jizō Bosatsu statues said to have been carved from a large cherry tree by [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] in 852 (Ninju 2). Originally enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}} in {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}}, they were redistributed during the Hōgen era to six entrance points to the capital by order of [[:en:Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori]]. ** {{ill|Edo Rokujizō|ja|江戸六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Rokujizō|1=Edo Roku Jizo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550993}} – Modeled after the Kyoto set, these are six copper Jizō statues constructed at various places in Edo between the Hōei and Kyōhō eras. ** {{ill|Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja|木津の六地蔵磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 木津の磨崖仏|1=Kizu Magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519200}} – Six carved Jizō figures on rock faces in Nishi-ku, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. ** {{ill|Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja|高野街道六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jizō statues established along the Kōya Kaidō route in Wakayama Prefecture during the Edo period. *** First Jizō: Shimizu area, Hashimoto City *** Second Jizō: Minamibaba area, Hashimoto City *** Third Jizō: Shigeno area, Kudoyama Town *** Fourth Jizō: Kawane area, Kudoyama Town *** Fifth Jizō: Sakumizu area, Kōya Town *** Sixth Jizō: Sakurachaya area, Kōya Town ** {{ill|Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|阿波六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Awa Six jizō pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657507}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Tokushima Prefecture (Awa Province). ** {{ill|Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|伊予六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16482459}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Ehime Prefecture (Iyo Province). * {{ill|Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja|六地蔵 (狂言)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A traditional Kyōgen play titled "Rokujizō."
; Place names in Japan * {{ill|Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja|六地蔵 (長柄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Nagara Town, Chōsei District, Chiba Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|ja|六地蔵町 (岡崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|1=Rokujizō-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215339}} – A locality in Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja|六地蔵 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Rittō City, Shiga Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}} – A former village in Kii District, Yamashiro Province (now Kyoto Prefecture). Associated with six wooden Jizō statues enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}}, located in Momoyama-chō, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Uji)|ja|六地蔵 (宇治市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Uji)|1=Rokujizō (Uji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56349096}} – A locality in Uji City, Kyoto Prefecture. Lies east of Yamashina River in the area historically part of Rokujizō Village. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja|六地蔵 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture
; Stations * {{ill|Rokujizō Station|ja|六地蔵駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Station|qq=}} – A station located on the border of Fushimi Ward (Kyoto City) and Uji City, operated by JR West, Keihan Electric Railway, and Kyoto Municipal Subway.

Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}}, they were redistributed during the Hōgen era to six entrance Associated with six wooden Jizō statues enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}}, located in Momoyama-chō, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Uji)|ja|六地蔵 (宇治市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Uji)|1=Rokujizō (Uji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56349096}} – A locality in Uji City, Kyoto Prefecture. Lies east of Yamashina River in the area historically part of Rokujizō Village. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja|六地蔵 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture
; Stations * {{ill|Rokujizō Station|ja|六地蔵駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Station|qq=}} – A station located on the border of Fushimi Ward (Kyoto City) and Uji City, operated by JR West, Keihan Electric Railway, and Kyoto Municipal Subway.

Tokushima Prefecture (Awa Province). ** {{ill|Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|伊予六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on (Kyōgen)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A traditional Kyōgen play titled "Rokujizō."
; Place names in Japan * {{ill|Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja|六地蔵 (長柄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Nagara Town, Chōsei District, Chiba Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|ja|六地蔵町 (岡崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|1=Rokujizō-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215339}} – A locality in Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja|六地蔵 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Rittō City, Shiga Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}} – A former village in Kii District, Yamashiro Province (now Kyoto Prefecture). Associated with six wooden Jizō statues enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}}, located in Momoyama-chō, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Uji)|ja|六地蔵 (宇治市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Uji)|1=Rokujizō (Uji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56349096}} – A locality in Uji City, Kyoto Prefecture. <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392304]] Wikidata name: Rokujizō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Six Jizō''' (六地蔵, Rokujizō)
* Refers to six statues of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] enshrined together, based on the Buddhist concept of the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]]. Also called {{ill|Rokutai Jizō|ja|六体地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokutai Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地蔵菩薩|1=Kṣitigarbha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q604687}}. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-tō|ja|六地蔵塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-tō|1=Rokujizō-tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392305}} – Stone towers carved with six Jizō images on their sides. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-ji|ja|六地蔵寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-ji|1=Rokujizo Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120882}} – A Shingon sect temple in Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture, enshrining six Jizō Bosatsu as its principal images. ** {{ill|Kyōto Rokujizō|ja|京都六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōto Rokujizō|1=Kyōto Rokujizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56350316}} – A set of six wooden Jizō Bosatsu statues said to have been carved from a large cherry tree by [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] in 852 (Ninju 2). Originally enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}} in {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}}, they were redistributed during the Hōgen era to six entrance points to the capital by order of [[:en:Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori]]. ** {{ill|Edo Rokujizō|ja|江戸六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Rokujizō|1=Edo Roku Jizo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550993}} – Modeled after the Kyoto set, these are six copper Jizō statues constructed at various places in Edo between the Hōei and Kyōhō eras. ** {{ill|Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja|木津の六地蔵磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 木津の磨崖仏|1=Kizu Magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519200}} – Six carved Jizō figures on rock faces in Nishi-ku, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. ** {{ill|Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja|高野街道六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jizō statues established along the Kōya Kaidō route in Wakayama Prefecture during the Edo period. *** First Jizō: Shimizu area, Hashimoto City *** Second Jizō: Minamibaba area, Hashimoto City *** Third Jizō: Shigeno area, Kudoyama Town *** Fourth Jizō: Kawane area, Kudoyama Town *** Fifth Jizō: Sakumizu area, Kōya Town *** Sixth Jizō: Sakurachaya area, Kōya Town ** {{ill|Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|阿波六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Awa Six jizō pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657507}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Tokushima Prefecture (Awa Province). ** {{ill|Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|伊予六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16482459}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Ehime Prefecture (Iyo Province). * {{ill|Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja|六地蔵 (狂言)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A traditional Kyōgen play titled "Rokujizō."
; Place names in Japan * {{ill|Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja|六地蔵 (長柄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Nagara Town, Chōsei District, Chiba Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|ja|六地蔵町 (岡崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|1=Rokujizō-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215339}} – A locality in Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja|六地蔵 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Rittō City, Shiga Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}} – A former village in Kii District, Yamashiro Province (now Kyoto Prefecture). Associated with six wooden Jizō statues enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}}, located in Momoyama-chō, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Uji)|ja|六地蔵 (宇治市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Uji)|1=Rokujizō (Uji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56349096}} – A locality in Uji City, Kyoto Prefecture. Lies east of Yamashina River in the area historically part of Rokujizō Village. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja|六地蔵 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture
; Stations * {{ill|Rokujizō Station|ja|六地蔵駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Station|qq=}} – A station located on the border of Fushimi Ward (Kyoto City) and Uji City, operated by JR West, Keihan Electric Railway, and Kyoto Municipal Subway.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392304]] Wikidata name: Rokujizō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Six Jizō''' (六地蔵, Rokujizō)
* Refers to six statues of [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Jizō Bosatsu]] enshrined together, based on the Buddhist concept of the [[:en:Six Paths|Six Realms of Existence]]. Also called {{ill|Rokutai Jizō|ja|六体地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokutai Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地蔵菩薩|1=Kṣitigarbha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q604687}}. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-tō|ja|六地蔵塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-tō|1=Rokujizō-tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392305}} – Stone towers carved with six Jizō images on their sides. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-ji|ja|六地蔵寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-ji|1=Rokujizo Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120882}} – A Shingon sect temple in Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture, enshrining six Jizō Bosatsu as its principal images. ** {{ill|Kyōto Rokujizō|ja|京都六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōto Rokujizō|1=Kyōto Rokujizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56350316}} – A set of six wooden Jizō Bosatsu statues said to have been carved from a large cherry tree by [[:en:Ono no Takamura|Ono no Takamura]] in 852 (Ninju 2). Originally enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}} in {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}}, they were redistributed during the Hōgen era to six entrance points to the capital by order of [[:en:Taira no Kiyomori|Taira no Kiyomori]]. ** {{ill|Edo Rokujizō|ja|江戸六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Rokujizō|1=Edo Roku Jizo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550993}} – Modeled after the Kyoto set, these are six copper Jizō statues constructed at various places in Edo between the Hōei and Kyōhō eras. ** {{ill|Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja|木津の六地蔵磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 木津の磨崖仏|1=Kizu Magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519200}} – Six carved Jizō figures on rock faces in Nishi-ku, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. ** {{ill|Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja|高野街道六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jizō statues established along the Kōya Kaidō route in Wakayama Prefecture during the Edo period. *** First Jizō: Shimizu area, Hashimoto City *** Second Jizō: Minamibaba area, Hashimoto City *** Third Jizō: Shigeno area, Kudoyama Town *** Fourth Jizō: Kawane area, Kudoyama Town *** Fifth Jizō: Sakumizu area, Kōya Town *** Sixth Jizō: Sakurachaya area, Kōya Town ** {{ill|Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|阿波六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Awa Six jizō pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657507}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Tokushima Prefecture (Awa Province). ** {{ill|Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|伊予六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16482459}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Ehime Prefecture (Iyo Province). * {{ill|Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja|六地蔵 (狂言)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A traditional Kyōgen play titled "Rokujizō."
; Place names in Japan * {{ill|Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja|六地蔵 (長柄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Nagara Town, Chōsei District, Chiba Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|ja|六地蔵町 (岡崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|1=Rokujizō-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215339}} – A locality in Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja|六地蔵 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Rittō City, Shiga Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}} – A former village in Kii District, Yamashiro Province (now Kyoto Prefecture). ; Stations * {{ill|Rokujizō Station|ja|六地蔵駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Station|qq=}} – A station located on the border of Fushimi Ward (Kyoto City) and Uji City, operated by JR West, Keihan Electric Railway, and Kyoto Municipal Subway.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11392304]] Wikidata name: Rokujizō [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Six Jizō''' (六地蔵, Rokujizō)
* Refers to six statues of {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}} enshrined together, based on the Buddhist concept of the {{ill|Six Paths|en|Six Paths|WD=Q1192450|lt=Six Realms of Existence}}. Also called {{ill|Rokutai Jizō|ja|六体地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokutai Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地蔵菩薩|1=Kṣitigarbha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q604687}}. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-tō|ja|六地蔵塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-tō|1=Rokujizō-tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392305}} – Stone towers carved with six Jizō images on their sides. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-ji|ja|六地蔵寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-ji|1=Rokujizo Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120882}} – A Shingon sect temple in Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture, enshrining six Jizō Bosatsu as its principal images. ** {{ill|Kyōto Rokujizō|ja|京都六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōto Rokujizō|1=Kyōto Rokujizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56350316}} – A set of six wooden Jizō Bosatsu statues said to have been carved from a large cherry tree by {{ill|Ono no Takamura|en|Ono no Takamura|WD=Q1139928|lt=Ono no Takamura}} in 852 (Ninju 2). Originally enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}} in {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}}, they were redistributed during the Hōgen era to six entrance points to the capital by order of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|en|Taira no Kiyomori|WD=Q281833|lt=Taira no Kiyomori}}. ** {{ill|Edo Rokujizō|ja|江戸六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Rokujizō|1=Edo Roku Jizo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550993}} – Modeled after the Kyoto set, these are six copper Jizō statues constructed at various places in Edo between the Hōei and Kyōhō eras. ** {{ill|Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja|木津の六地蔵磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 木津の磨崖仏|1=Kizu Magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519200}} – Six carved Jizō figures on rock faces in Nishi-ku, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. ** {{ill|Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja|高野街道六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jizō statues established along the Kōya Kaidō route in Wakayama Prefecture during the Edo period. *** First Jizō: Shimizu area, Hashimoto City *** Second Jizō: Minamibaba area, Hashimoto City *** Third Jizō: Shigeno area, Kudoyama Town *** Fourth Jizō: Kawane area, Kudoyama Town *** Fifth Jizō: Sakumizu area, Kōya Town *** Sixth Jizō: Sakurachaya area, Kōya Town ** {{ill|Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|阿波六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Awa Six jizō pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657507}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Tokushima Prefecture (Awa Province). ** {{ill|Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|伊予六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16482459}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Ehime Prefecture (Iyo Province). * {{ill|Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja|六地蔵 (狂言)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A traditional Kyōgen play titled "Rokujizō."
; Place names in Japan * {{ill|Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja|六地蔵 (長柄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Nagara Town, Chōsei District, Chiba Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|ja|六地蔵町 (岡崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|1=Rokujizō-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215339}} – A locality in Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja|六地蔵 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Rittō City, Shiga Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}} – A former village in Kii District, Yamashiro Province (now Kyoto Prefecture). Associated with six wooden Jizō statues enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}}, located in Momoyama-chō, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Uji)|ja|六地蔵 (宇治市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Uji)|1=Rokujizō (Uji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56349096}} – A locality in Uji City, Kyoto Prefecture. Lies east of Yamashina River in the area historically part of Rokujizō Village. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja|六地蔵 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture
; Stations * {{ill|Rokujizō Station|ja|六地蔵駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Station|qq=}} – A station located on the border of Fushimi Ward (Kyoto City) and Uji City, operated by JR West, Keihan Electric Railway, and Kyoto Municipal Subway.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:六地蔵]]
'''Six Jizō''' (六地蔵, Rokujizō)
* Refers to six statues of {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Jizō Bosatsu}} enshrined together, based on the Buddhist concept of the {{ill|Six Paths|en|Six Paths|WD=Q1192450|lt=Six Realms of Existence}}. Also called {{ill|Rokutai Jizō|ja|六体地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokutai Jizō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 地蔵菩薩|1=Kṣitigarbha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q604687}}. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-tō|ja|六地蔵塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-tō|1=Rokujizō-tō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392305}} – Stone towers carved with six Jizō images on their sides. ** {{ill|Rokujizō-ji|ja|六地蔵寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-ji|1=Rokujizo Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22120882}} – A Shingon sect temple in Mito City, Ibaraki Prefecture, enshrining six Jizō Bosatsu as its principal images. ** {{ill|Kyōto Rokujizō|ja|京都六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kyōto Rokujizō|1=Kyōto Rokujizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56350316}} – A set of six wooden Jizō Bosatsu statues said to have been carved from a large cherry tree by {{ill|Ono no Takamura|en|Ono no Takamura|WD=Q1139928|lt=Ono no Takamura}} in 852 (Ninju 2). Originally enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}} in {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}}, they were redistributed during the Hōgen era to six entrance points to the capital by order of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|en|Taira no Kiyomori|WD=Q281833|lt=Taira no Kiyomori}}. ** {{ill|Edo Rokujizō|ja|江戸六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Rokujizō|1=Edo Roku Jizo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550993}} – Modeled after the Kyoto set, these are six copper Jizō statues constructed at various places in Edo between the Hōei and Kyōhō eras. ** {{ill|Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja|木津の六地蔵磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 木津の磨崖仏|1=Kizu Magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519200}} – Six carved Jizō figures on rock faces in Nishi-ku, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. ** {{ill|Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja|高野街道六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jizō statues established along the Kōya Kaidō route in Wakayama Prefecture during the Edo period. *** First Jizō: Shimizu area, Hashimoto City *** Second Jizō: Minamibaba area, Hashimoto City *** Third Jizō: Shigeno area, Kudoyama Town *** Fourth Jizō: Kawane area, Kudoyama Town *** Fifth Jizō: Sakumizu area, Kōya Town *** Sixth Jizō: Sakurachaya area, Kōya Town ** {{ill|Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|阿波六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Awa Six jizō pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657507}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Tokushima Prefecture (Awa Province). ** {{ill|Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|伊予六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16482459}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Ehime Prefecture (Iyo Province). * {{ill|Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja|六地蔵 (狂言)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A traditional Kyōgen play titled "Rokujizō."
; Place names in Japan * {{ill|Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja|六地蔵 (長柄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Nagara Town, Chōsei District, Chiba Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|ja|六地蔵町 (岡崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|1=Rokujizō-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215339}} – A locality in Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja|六地蔵 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Rittō City, Shiga Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Rokujizō Village|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}} – A former village in Kii District, Yamashiro Province (now Kyoto Prefecture). Associated with six wooden Jizō statues enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}}, located in Momoyama-chō, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Uji)|ja|六地蔵 (宇治市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Uji)|1=Rokujizō (Uji)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56349096}} – A locality in Uji City, Kyoto Prefecture. Lies east of Yamashina River in the area historically part of Rokujizō Village. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja|六地蔵 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture
; Stations * {{ill|Rokujizō Station|ja|六地蔵駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Station|qq=}} – A station located on the border of Fushimi Ward (Kyoto City) and Uji City, operated by JR West, Keihan Electric Railway, and Kyoto Municipal Subway.
==See Also== {{Commonscat|Six Jizo}} * {{Prefix|Rokujizō}} * {{Intitle|Rokujizō}}
{{disambiguation}}

Originally enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}} in {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Q109361344|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}}, they were redistributed during the Hōgen era to six entrance points to the capital by order of {{ill|Taira no Kiyomori|en|Taira no Kiyomori|WD=Q281833|lt=Taira no Kiyomori|1=Taira no Kiyomori}}. ** {{ill|Edo Rokujizō|ja|江戸六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo Rokujizō|1=Edo Roku Jizō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11550993}} – Modeled after the Kyoto set, these are six copper Jizō statues constructed at various places in Edo between the Hōei and Kyōhō eras. ** {{ill|Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja|木津の六地蔵磨崖仏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kizu no Rokujizō Magaibutsu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 木津の磨崖仏|1=Kizu Magaibutsu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11519200}} – Six carved Jizō figures on rock faces in Nishi-ku, Kobe City, Hyōgo Prefecture. ** {{ill|Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja|高野街道六地蔵|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōya Kaidō Rokujizō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – Jizō statues established along the Kōya Kaidō route in Wakayama Prefecture during the Edo period. *** First Jizō: Shimizu area, Hashimoto City *** Second Jizō: Minamibaba area, Hashimoto City *** Third Jizō: Shigeno area, Kudoyama Town *** Fourth Jizō: Kawane area, Kudoyama Town *** Fifth Jizō: Sakumizu area, Kōya Town *** Sixth Jizō: Sakurachaya area, Kōya Town ** {{ill|Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|阿波六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Awa Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Awa Six jizō pilgrimage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11657507}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Tokushima Prefecture (Awa Province). ** {{ill|Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|ja|伊予六地蔵霊場|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Rokujizō Pilgrimage|1=Q16482459|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q16482459}} – A pilgrimage of six Jizō Bosatsu sites in Ehime Prefecture (Iyo Province). * {{ill|Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja|六地蔵 (狂言)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Kyōgen)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A traditional Kyōgen play titled "Rokujizō."
; Place names in Japan * {{ill|Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja|六地蔵 (長柄町)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Nagara)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Nagara Town, Chōsei District, Chiba Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|ja|六地蔵町 (岡崎市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō-chō (Okazaki)|1=Rokujizō-chō, Okazaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17215339}} – A locality in Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja|六地蔵 (栗東市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Rittō)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Rittō City, Shiga Prefecture * {{ill|Rokujizō Village|ja|六地蔵村|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Village|1=Q109361344|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q109361344}} – A former village in Kii District, Yamashiro Province (now Kyoto Prefecture). Associated with six wooden Jizō statues enshrined at {{ill|Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|ja|大善寺 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daizen-ji (Kyoto)|1=Daizenji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11433633}}, located in Momoyama-chō, Fushimi Ward, Kyoto City. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Uji)|ja|六地蔵 (宇治市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Uji)|1=Q56349096|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56349096}} – A locality in Uji City, Kyoto Prefecture. Lies east of Yamashina River in the area historically part of Rokujizō Village. * {{ill|Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja|六地蔵 (豊岡市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō (Toyooka)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A locality in Toyooka City, Hyōgo Prefecture
; Stations * {{ill|Rokujizō Station|ja|六地蔵駅|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujizō Station|qq=}} – A station located on the border of Fushimi Ward (Kyoto City) and Uji City, operated by JR West, Keihan Electric Railway, and Kyoto Municipal Subway.
